If you want 'strictness' limited to a particular semester or academic year, add a filter on course_sections.academic_year and/or course_sections.semester_type.","num_grades is provided so downstream consumers can exclude professors with very small sample sizes (e.g., num_grades < N) if desired; this plan does not automatically filter by sample size.","Visual axes reference the exact output field aliases (professor_name, avg_grade) as required."]},"attempt":2,"maxAttempts":4,"previousAttempt":{"sql":"WITH professor_grades AS (
  SELECT
    p.id AS professor_id,
    CONCAT(p.first_name, ' ', p.last_name) AS professor_name,
    AVG(g.exam_score) AS avg_grade,
    COUNT(g.id) AS num_grades
  FROM public.grades g
  JOIN public.enrollments e ON g.enrollment_id = e.id
  JOIN public.course_sections cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
  JOIN public.professors p ON cs.professor_id = p.id
  WHERE g.exam_score IS NOT NULL
    AND cs.professor_id IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY p.id, p.first_name, p.last_name
)
SELECT
  professor_id,
  professor_name,
  avg_grade,
  num_grades
FROM professor_grades
ORDER BY avg_grade ASC, professor_name ASC;","validationIssues":["No sample-size filtering: the visualization includes professors with very small num_grades (e.g., 1–2), which can make 'strictness' misleading — consider filtering or highlighting low-sample professors (e.g., num_grades >= N).","Ordering is applied both in the SQL (ORDER BY avg_grade ASC) and in Vega-Lite (encoding.x.sort by avg_grade ASC); this is consistent but redundant.","If the professor list grows long, x-axis labels may overlap despite labelAngle:-40 — consider horizontal scrolling, wrapping, or a vertical bar chart as a usability improvement."]},"historySummary":"Attempt 1: passed","latest":{"sqlDraft":{"reasoning":"Compute per-professor average of numeric exam_score using confirmed join path (grades -> enrollments -> course_sections -> professors); return professor id, full name, average score and count, ordered with lowest averages first so strictest graders appear at top.","sql":"WITH professor_grades AS (

  JOIN public.professors p ON cs.professor_id = p.id

  GROUP BY p.id, p.first_name, p.last_name
)

visualization.title: Professors ordered by average exam score (lowest = strictest)

resultRaw.sql:
WITH professor_grades AS (
  SELECT
    p.id AS professor_id,
    CONCAT(p.first_name, ' ', p.last_name) AS professor_name,
    AVG(g.exam_score) AS avg_grade,
    COUNT(g.id) AS num_grades
  FROM public.grades g
  JOIN public.enrollments e ON g.enrollment_id = e.id
  JOIN public.course_sections cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
  JOIN public.professors p ON cs.professor_id = p.id
  WHERE g.exam_score IS NOT NULL
    AND cs.professor_id IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY p.id, p.first_name, p.last_name
)
SELECT
  professor_id,
  professor_name,
  avg_grade,
  num_grades
FROM professor_grades
ORDER BY avg_grade ASC, professor_name ASC;

issues: No sample-size filtering: the visualization includes professors with very small num_grades (e.g., 1–2), which can make 'strictness' misleading — consider filtering or highlighting low-sample professors (e.g., num_grades >= N).
Ordering is applied both in the SQL (ORDER BY avg_grade ASC) and in Vega-Lite (encoding.x.sort by avg_grade ASC); this is consistent but redundant.
If the professor list grows long, x-axis labels may overlap despite labelAngle:-40 — consider horizontal scrolling, wrapping, or a vertical bar chart as a usability improvement.

visualization.title: Professors ordered by average exam score (lowest = strictest)

result.sql:
WITH professor_grades AS (
  SELECT
    p.id AS professor_id,
    CONCAT(p.first_name, ' ', p.last_name) AS professor_name,
    AVG(g.exam_score) AS avg_grade,
    COUNT(g.id) AS num_grades
  FROM public.grades g
  JOIN public.enrollments e ON g.enrollment_id = e.id
  JOIN public.course_sections cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
  JOIN public.professors p ON cs.professor_id = p.id
  WHERE g.exam_score IS NOT NULL
    AND cs.professor_id IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY p.id, p.first_name, p.last_name
)
SELECT
  professor_id,
  professor_name,
  avg_grade,
  num_grades
FROM professor_grades
ORDER BY avg_grade ASC, professor_name ASC;

issues: No sample-size filtering: the visualization includes professors with very small num_grades (e.g., 1–2), which can make 'strictness' misleading — consider filtering or highlighting low-sample professors (e.g., num_grades >= N).
Ordering is applied both in the SQL (ORDER BY avg_grade ASC) and in Vega-Lite (encoding.x.sort by avg_grade ASC); this is consistent but redundant.
If the professor list grows long, x-axis labels may overlap despite labelAngle:-40 — consider horizontal scrolling, wrapping, or a vertical bar chart as a usability improvement.
